content:
What are Technology Readiness Levels (TRL 7-9) in Demonstration?

TRL 7: System Prototype in Operational Environment

Prototype near or at planned operational system. It requires demonstration of an actual system prototype in an operational environment (e.g., in an aircraft, in a vehicle, or in space).

TRL 8: System Complete and Qualified

Technology has been proven to work in its final form and under expected conditions. In almost all cases, this TRL represents the end of true system development.

TRL 9: Actual System Proven in Successful Operations

Actual application of the technology in its final form and under mission conditions, such as those encountered in operational test and evaluation.

What are the Major Stage 3 Demonstration & Pilot Programs?

SDTC - Sustainable Development Technology Canada

  • Pre-commercial scale demonstration
  • Real-world operating conditions
  • Performance validation at scale
  • Environmental impact measurement
  • Up to 33% project funding (repayable)
  • Multi-year project support
  • Technical and business advisory
  • Strategic partnership facilitation

Clean Growth Program - Innovation & Scale-up

  • Industrial demonstration projects
  • First-of-kind installations
  • Scale-up manufacturing pilots
  • Commercial validation projects
  • Up to 50% of eligible costs
  • Repayable contribution structure
  • Partnership development support
  • Market access facilitation

Sector-Specific Pilot Programs

  • Agricultural technology pilots
  • Manufacturing automation demos
  • Energy system demonstrations
  • Transportation technology pilots
  • Real customer/user testing
  • Performance data collection
  • Business model validation
  • Market feedback and iteration

Strategic Innovation Fund (SIF) - Demonstration Stream

  • Significant economic impact
  • Strategic sector alignment
  • Large-scale demonstration
  • Job creation potential
  • Repayable contributions
  • Multi-year project timelines
  • Co-investment requirements
  • Performance milestones

What Does Stage 3 Demonstration & Pilot Funding Cover?

🎯 Stage 3 Outcomes

How Do You Move From Pilot to Commercial in Stage 3?

Pilot Deployment

Data Collection

Regulatory Approval

First Commercial Sale

Case Study: How Does a CleanTech Pivot Work?

CleanFlow Systems (Hypothetical)

Industrial wastewater treatment system for mining operations.

Secured $3M SDTC grant (33% project cost) to install a full-scale pilot at a Partner Mine in Northern Ontario.

Pilot data showed 95% efficiency but high energy use. Used $500k Clean Growth funding to re-engineer power systems.

Partner Mine converted pilot to permanent installation. Signed 3 new mining contracts based on proven data.

What are the Strategies for Stage 3 Demonstration Funding Success?

✅ Best Practices

❌ Common Mistakes

Official Government Resources

What are Common Questions About Demonstration & Pilot Funding?

Quick answers to the most frequently asked questions about SDTC, Clean Growth, and pilot project funding.

How much SDTC funding is available?

Up to $15M covering 33% of eligible project costs

Do I need a pilot customer?

Yes, a letter of intent or pilot agreement is typically required

Is SDTC funding repayable?

Structured as non-repayable contributions with success-based conditions

What does TRL 7-9 mean?

System prototype in real-world operation through commercial deployment

How long does SDTC approval take?

6-9 months from application to approval during funding rounds

Do I give up equity?

No, all Stage 3 programs are non-dilutive government funding

📚 Related Guides

Build prototypes and validate technology with IRAP and SR&ED

Stage 4: Commercialization & Scale-Up

Market launch funding through SIF and export programs

faq.question: What is the application deadline for Stage 3: Demonstration & Pilot Funding Canada  | SDTC & Clean Growth | Up to  Project Funding in 2026?
faq.answer: Deadlines typically vary by specific program tier. However, for 2026, many initiatives operate on a rolling intake basis until funds are depleted. Check the official portal for specific cutoffs.

faq.question: How much funding can I get through Stage 3: Demonstration & Pilot Funding Canada  | SDTC & Clean Growth | Up to  Project Funding?
faq.answer: Funding amounts range depending on business size and scope. Top awards can reach $15M.

faq.question: Do I have to give up equity for this program?
faq.answer: No. Government grants and subsidies are non-dilutive, meaning you retain 100% ownership of your company.

expertTip.title: Pre-Revenue Eligibility
expertTip.content: Demonstration funds like SDTC are specifically for pre-revenue technologies. If you are already selling the product commercially, you are too late for this stage.
